blurb:
Cody's last family loved him. They really did. But a Cattle Dog mix with a working-breed brain needs a job, or at least enough daily exercise that he feels like he has one. Two months in, it just wasn't the right fit, and he ended up back at Mostly Mutts. No hard feelings. He just needs someone who's actually going to run him.

He's three years old, about 33 lbs, and he went through the Mostly Mutts Prison Pups training program before his first adoption. So the foundation is there. Volunteers describe him as strong and lovable with a sweet nature, and he proved he could handle doggie daycare during his trial adoption, which is a good sign for a dog with his energy level. He's been friendly with other dogs he's met at the shelter and in foster settings.

He's got some mouthy playfulness that's common for Cattle Dogs, and he's wiggly when he's excited, so toddlers would be a tough pairing. Older kids he's met have gone fine. If you run, hike, bike, or just genuinely enjoy a dog that wants to be moving with you most of the day, Cody is going to match that energy perfectly.

Find his full profile at mostlymutts.org and reach out to set up a meet. Mostly Mutts is located in Kennesaw.
scoring_notes:
• Return adoption after two months plus a prior training program signals a dog who has been cycling through the system and genuinely needs the right match
• Working-breed-needs-a-job narrative targets an active reader demographic that often self-selects as committed long-term owners
• Two returns to the shelter in his history combined with training investment already made creates meaningful urgency for placement

blurb:
During his bath at the shelter, Wilson drank directly from the sprayer and handed out kisses the whole time. That pretty much tells you everything you need to know about this guy.

He's three years old, about 46 lbs, a Bulldog-Boxer mix, and he came into Mostly Mutts from animal control. He went through their Prison Pups immersive training program and is currently living with a foster family while he waits. He's done well with kids of various ages in the foster home, calm and affectionate when they come up to pet him. He's also good with other dogs, so much so that his trainer actually uses him to help teach other dogs how to play nicely.

Fair warning: he's a talker. He vocalizes when he plays, with people and with other dogs. It's not aggression, just enthusiasm. If your current dog doesn't mind a vocal playmate, they'd probably get along fine. Cats are a no, and toddlers would be a tough fit just because he's strong and moves fast. But older kids, active adults, and dog-friendly households? He's going to fit right in.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. Visit mostlymutts.org to see Wilson's full profile and start the adoption process.
scoring_notes:
• Bath-time sprayer anecdote is the kind of single image that gets shared and drives adoption inquiries on its own
• Prison Pups graduate who mentors other dogs positions him as a standout character, not just another shelter dog
• Animal control origin plus foster-home wait suggests a moderate stay with enough urgency to justify a feature

blurb:
She came back to Mostly Mutts after nine months in a home, and when she returned she was shut down, scared, and barely recognizable from the dog she'd been before. That was then. Cecelia is three years old, about 37 lbs, a Terrier-Boxer mix, and she is doing great in her foster home now. She just needed time to decompress.

Once she trusts you, she's a lap dog. Full body wags. Crawls right up on you and acts like she's been there her whole life. She's already working through the Mostly Mutts University training program and has sit, stay, come, and leave it down. Her foster family describes her as the perfect housemate, not the high-energy handful her previous home thought she was.

She does need some patience up front. Reaching over her head or grabbing her collar too quickly will spook her, so you'll want to let her come to you on her own timeline rather than rushing it. She'd do best as your only dog at home, and Mostly Mutts recommends calmer kids 15 and up given her history with new people. If that sounds like your house, she's worth a visit.

Mostly Mutts is in Kennesaw. You can browse her full profile and start an inquiry at mostlymutts.org. Reach out to ask about scheduling a meet.
scoring_notes:
• Returned-dog redemption arc is among the most emotionally resonant stories a shelter newsletter can tell
• Trained, lap-dog affectionate, and thriving in foster signals she is ready now despite a complicated history
• Nine-month prior adoption plus decompress recovery period implies meaningful cumulative shelter time and real urgency

blurb:
Calico kittens are pretty hard to walk past, and Croissant is not going to make it any easier. She's two and a half months old, about two pounds, came in from animal control with her siblings, and is currently in that shy-but-thawing phase that most kittens go through when the shelter is new and the people are strangers.

The volunteers at Mostly Mutts are already working on it. She likes playing with her siblings, she's starting to warm up to being petted, and the consensus seems to be that once she decides you're safe, the cuddling and playing will follow pretty quickly. She's litter trained, already spayed, microchipped, and current on all her shots.

She does great with other cats, so if you've got one at home who could use a friend, she's worth considering. Gentle kids are welcome. Dogs are unknown at this point. Adoption fee is $175. She's at the Mostly Mutts shelter in Kennesaw, and you can head in to meet her.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Calico coloring is a proven visual hook that drives clicks and walk-in traffic at shelters
• Fully vetted and spayed on arrival makes her a plug-and-play adoption with zero hidden costs
• Backstory is thinner than sibling candidates, relying on breed appeal rather than a compelling narrative arc

blurb:
Cannoli came in from animal control a little while back, and he's been doing what shy kittens do: watching everything, warming up slowly, and letting the volunteers earn his trust one gentle interaction at a time. He's a two-and-a-half-month-old tuxedo boy, about two pounds, and according to the people spending time with him, he's already starting to enjoy the attention.

He's not the run-up-and-jump-on-you type yet. He plays with his siblings, is getting more comfortable with people every day, and the volunteers are putting in the work to show him that humans are worth trusting. Kittens like this tend to flip fast once they decide you're okay. Then they don't leave you alone.

He gets along great with other cats and would probably thrive with a buddy at home. Gentle kids are fine. Dogs are unknown. He's already neutered, microchipped, and up to date on shots, so the hard stuff is done. Adoption fee is $175. He's at the shelter in Kennesaw and you can go meet him in person.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Already neutered, microchipped, and vaccinated removes every friction point from the adoption decision
• Slow-to-trust kitten who flips fast is a relatable underdog story with a satisfying payoff for the right reader
• Arrived recently from animal control with no strong shelter-time signals, limiting urgency score

blurb:
She was born in someone's garage to a feral mom, got a rough start with an upper respiratory infection, and has spent the last few weeks just trying to feel better. Now she does. Souffle is a two-and-a-half-month-old tuxedo kitten, about two pounds, and by all accounts she's been a little trooper through the whole thing.

The sniffles are gone and the kitten energy is back. She's chasing toys again, pouncing on shadows, and rediscovering all the things kittens are supposed to be doing. She's also still very much into cuddle time, which makes sense given that cuddles were her main form of medicine for a while. Affectionate and playful, litter trained, and genuinely sweet.

She does great with other kittens and would probably love a cat companion at home. Dogs are unknown, and kids are fine as long as they're gentle with something this small. She's living in the Kitty Room at Mostly Mutts right now, so you can walk in and meet her. The adoption fee is $175.
shelter_hours: Please check mostlymutts.org for current hours
scoring_notes:
• Tuxedo kitten with cuddle-plus-play combo is a high-conversion adoption profile for any audience
• Feral-born survivor who battled illness and bounced back is a feel-good arc readers root for
• Recent arrival with only a few weeks at shelter limits urgency, but youth means placement window is short
